input.question: Rank all available cities by population density (people per km2). Which three are densest and what are their exact densities?

messages.content: Rank all available cities by population density (people per km2). Which three are densest and what are their exact densities?

Here are the population densities (people per km2) for the available cities:

- Tokyo: 6362.81 people/km2
- New York: 10549.17 people/km2
- Paris: 20580.95 people/km2
- London: 5713.74 people/km2
- Sydney: 429.50 people/km2
- Mumbai: 34273.63 people/km2
- Sao Paulo: 8106.51 people/km2

The three densest cities are:
1. Mumbai with 34273.63 people/km2
2. Paris with 20580.95 people/km2
3. New York with 10549.17 people/km2

- 'research_first' — needs more lookups before any calculations   (e.g., multi-entity comparisons, cross-referencing country+city data)
- 'analysis_first' — facts are sufficient, needs computation/conversion   (e.g., pure math, simple unit conversion on already-known values)
- 'research_and_analysis' — needs both more lookups AND heavy computation   (e.g., multi-hop chains: look up -> compute -> look up more -> compute)

Respond with ONLY one of: research_first, analysis_first, research_and_analysis
